sections.paragraph:
κέλευσμα, ατος, τό (s. κελεύω; a summons to carry out a procedure, e.g. battle engagement, rowing, hunting: Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; Sb 4279, 3 [I a.d.]; Pr 30:27; TestSol 6:6 P; GrBar ins [κελεύματι]; Philo, Abr. 116; Jos., Ant. 17, 140; 199; on the spelling s. B-D-F §70, 3) signal, (cry of) command (Hdt. 4, 141 of a signal for engagement in battle) ὁ κύριος ἐν κ. καταβήσεται the Lord will come down with a cry of command = when the command is given 1 Th 4:16 (cp. on the κέλευσμα of God, Galen XIX 179 K. τοῦ δημιουργοῦ; Philo, Praem. 117 and Descensus Mariae in Rtzst., Poim. 5, 3).—DELG s.v. κελεύω. TW.

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

Rtzst., Poim. Rtzst., Poim. = Poimandres

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 538
